news.headline: After Andy Burnham’s Win, the UK Is Set to Test How Far Charisma Can Shift Electoral Dynamics
news.summary: The governing Labour Party may oust Prime Minister Keir Starmer in favor of the more popular Andy Burnham — who will still face all the same challenges.

news.headline: German journalist held in Syria back home, family says
news.summary: Eva Maria Michelmann's brother says she had been held in the Syrian city of Raqqa since January.

news.headline: India’s revolution in women’s cricket
news.summary: In cricket-obsessed India, a women’s World Cup win and a glitzy new league are transforming the lives of female players.

news.headline: Drone use expands in Sudan – and kills at least 1,000 civilians in 2026

news.headline: Why are so many Brits in Paris for Fête de la Musique?
news.summary:
Block parties all night, live music till early in the morning, and... British tourists?
 
In 2025, Paris's annual Fête de la Musique, the 'Festival of Music' marking the advent of summer, saw a wave of young Brits crossing the Channel to take part in the revelry.

news.headline: Suspect arrested after man attacked with rock in alleged attempted robbery
news.summary:
Hong Kong police have arrested a man in connection with an attempted robbery at Kai Tak Cruise Terminal, where the victim was allegedly attacked with a rock early on Saturday.
Police said that a 36‑year‑old man reported that he had been assaulted while parking his car at the terminal at about 4.10am

news.headline: Canadian beer prices test German fans' thirst at FIFA World Cup
news.summary: The FIFA World Cup is in full swing, including in Canada. Toronto is hosting Saturday's match between Germany and the Ivory Coast. The German supporters are there and they’re angry – according to them, the price of beer is far too high. Story by Daniel Quinlan and Antoine Fenaux.

news.headline: Beijing planning more surveys east of Taiwan to assert sovereignty, state media reports
news.summary:
Beijing will make maritime surveys in the waters east of Taiwan a routine matter as it seeks to assert its sovereignty following talks between Japan and the Philippines, state media reported on Friday.

news.headline: At least 30 deaths at DR Congo camp show Ebola could be spreading fast
news.summary:
At least 30 people have died since the start of May in one camp for displaced civilians in northeastern Congo, a death rate that camp officials said was unprecedented, with some confirmed to have died from Ebola in a sign the disease could be spreading fast there.
